evals.expected_output: AuthManager migrated to Auth0.Android v4: SecureCredentialsManager built from an AuthenticationAPIClient, SSOCredentials.expiresIn updated to expiresAt, prerequisites (minSdk 26, Java 17) raised, dependency updated to the latest v4 release, building successfully, with a summary of behavioral changes.
expectations: Verifies a clean git state and/or creates a backup branch before making any code changes
Detects the current version as 3.19.0 from build.gradle.kts
Resolves the target version dynamically — auto-resolves the latest v4 release (e.g. 4.0.0-beta.1) via the GitHub releases API, or validates a provided version argument
Fetches the v4 SDK Kotlin source (SecureCredentialsManager.kt, SSOCredentials.kt, etc.) from GitHub before applying changes — not from memory or a guide alone
Updates the com.auth0.android:auth0 dependency from 3.19.0 to a v4 tag
Rebuilds SecureCredentialsManager construction to pass an AuthenticationAPIClient instead of an Auth0 instance (§7.7)
Renames SSOCredentials.expiresIn to expiresAt and updates the return type from Int to Date (§7.6)
Reads the project source to identify which APIs are used, and does NOT apply changes for APIs the project never calls (e.g. no PasskeyAuthProvider, UsersAPIClient, or MFA changes)
Surfaces the behavioral changes that need no code change: minTtl default 0 to 60s (§8.1) and clearCredentials() now clearing all storage (§8.3)
Does not introduce client_secret in the native app
Does not downgrade credential storage from SecureCredentialsManager to plain SharedPreferences
Preserves existing functionality (getCredentials, SSO expiry, logout all still work)
Attempts a ./gradlew build to verify the migration compiles
Presents a migration summary after completion
